# ForkMind

> ForkMind is a local-first, MIT-licensed developer tool that treats AI context
> windows like a Git repository. It runs a proxy in front of any
> OpenAI-compatible LLM API (free by default via Ollama), records every LLM
> call as a node in a content-addressed Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) on disk,
> and provides branching, regression testing, and encrypted context offloading
> ("context capsules"). No cloud, no account, no telemetry.

Key facts:

- Install: `npm install -g forkmind` or `npx forkmind start` (npm package
  `forkmind`, current version 0.3.0). Proxy + dashboard run on localhost:4500.
- Works with any OpenAI-compatible provider (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq,
  OpenRouter, Together, LM Studio, vLLM) from any language: set the client
  base URL to `http://localhost:4500/v1`.
- Storage is plain JSON under a local `.forkmind/` directory; node ids are
  deterministic (`sha256(request + parentId)`, first 12 hex chars).
- **Branching**: fork an alternative prompt or model from any historical turn
  in the dashboard; only that subtree re-runs.
- **Regression testing**: pin a known-good output as a baseline, replay it
  after prompt/model changes, and fail CI on drift (`forkmind regression`).
- **Context capsules** (v0.2.0+): save conversation context as an immutable,
  acyclic, content-addressed DAG snapshot, encrypted with AES-256-GCM (keys
  stored outside the data directory in `~/.forkmind-keys/`), then remove it
  from the live model window. Restore in full or per segment. The contract is
  save → verify on disk → only then compact. Deletion is crypto-shredding:
  the capsule key is destroyed first and the id is tombstoned, so ciphertext
  in backups stays unreadable and identical content cannot resurrect the id.
- **RAID** (v0.3.0+): Redundant Array of Independent DAGs — capsules mirror
  to extra filesystem targets (ciphertext + manifests only; keys are never
  replicated) with self-healing restore on primary loss or corruption and
  tombstone propagation on sync.
- **MCP server** (`forkmind mcp`, stdio): tools `forkmind_recent`,
  `forkmind_get_node`, `forkmind_lineage`, `forkmind_children`,
  `forkmind_search`, `forkmind_stats`, `forkmind_context_save`,
  `forkmind_context_list`, `forkmind_context_digest`,
  `forkmind_context_restore`, `forkmind_context_forget`,
  `forkmind_context_replicas` — agents can query their own history and
  archive/restore their own context mid-task.
- **Claude Code plugin**: `/plugin marketplace add Medhovarsh/forkmind` then
  `/plugin install forkmind` — ships the `forkmind` skill, the
  `forkmind-archivist` context-offloading skill and subagent, the
  `forkmind-debugger` subagent, and the auto-wired MCP server.
- ForkMind never modifies the LLM, its weights, or its KV cache: everything
  happens in what the client sends.

- [README (full documentation)](https://github.com/Medhovarsh/forkmind#readme)
- [Context-offload agent design](https://github.com/Medhovarsh/forkmind/blob/master/docs/design/context-offload-agent.md)
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/forkmind)
- [Landing page](https://medhovarsh.github.io/forkmind/)
